mentions.note: Sticky, enterprise-focused B2B AI business with a faster path to profitability than OpenAI, but a rumored $350 billion valuation (30-40x sales) is a monster price to pay.
Claude Code emerging as enterprise software favorite; practical B2B AI solution; potential mega-IPO candidate at $350B valuation later in 2026.

Private company; Cramer mentioned he has seen rival AI products created at fraction of cost using Anthropic's platform; not enough conviction to recommend but technology competitive threat to enterprise software vendors.
New AI tools for legal, finance, HR, IT, marketing displacing expensive enterprise software vendors; revolutionary code-writing capabilities replacing coders.
Destroyer of software valuations through aggressive press releases claiming disruption capability. Taking down IBM (13% loss), Workday, entire enterprise software sector through fear narrative alone.
Valued at $380B in recent fundraising; AI arms race with OpenAI eating enterprise software market share; disrupting payroll and traditional software.
Private company; CEO Arthur Mensch discussed at GTC as canvas for AI applications; not yet public.

20%+ of Fundrise Innovation Fund holdings; called 'king of enterprise AI' by Cramer; frontier model driving enterprise transformation; major beneficiary of OpenAI's $120B round ecosystem (held by Fundrise since early rounds at lower valuations).
Private company making unfounded cyber security claims vs. CrowdStrike reality; narrative beats truth in market near-term; dangerous positioning.
Private company; new agentic AI model creating false market fears about cybersecurity obsolescence—reality is opposite.
Valuation $380B; mega-IPO in pipeline; could draw significant capital from broader market.
AI leader among creches; part of winning hardware/AI camp; Cramer notes these AI innovators will be joined by hardware users (Amazon, Meta, Alphabet) in pantheon of greatness.
100X data shows Claude at highest future usage intent ever tracked (40% of users increasing usage); beating ChatGPT decisively on usability and features; Anthropic is Nvidia's best customer.
Fourth-layer AI model provider; major AWS partnership; pre-IPO but key ecosystem component; spending heavily on infrastructure.

Currently $400B valuation; enterprise-focused (sticky, not fickle); possibly heading to $1T IPO; high institutional hunger; closest to profitability of trio; capital drain risk if launches with OpenAI/SpaceX.
$900B valuation in latest funding round, seeking new raise. Combined mega-IPO wave could suck $375B+ from market in span of months—7-8 years of normal IPO volume compressed.
Major AI customer of AWS/infrastructure providers; driving data center build-out demand for compute capacity.
Model builder announced major GPU order from Nvidia; part of AI revolution layer 4 (pre-IPO private; no price data).
Soon-to-be-public AI leader disrupting enterprise software with cheaper alternatives; #2 on CNBC Disruptor 50; hugely profitable based on Q2 preview.
Filed to go public today with $14B Amazon backing; listed as key Nvidia client alongside OpenAI and SpaceX; pre-IPO transition, no price data available.

B2B AI play with lucrative revenue streams; if only big IPO of 2026 would be all-in; sandwiched between SpaceX and OpenAI reduces enthusiasm; concerns about supply glut and potential underpricing in crowded queue.
Real enterprise revenue growing rapidly, but astronomical private valuations lack public market anchor; await IPO test.
Claude showing extraordinary 40% intent-to-use-more vs only 6% less—unmatched adoption score; pricing power evident; the deal to own.
Doing incredibly well; fantastic growth company with great product (Claude). Put in for as much stock as you can when IPO happens. Next two AI IPOs are OpenAI and Anthropic.
Pre-IPO/private AI company; doing incredibly well with Claude product; fantastic growth company; Cramer wants club members to put in for as much stock as possible.
Powerful new AI finance competitor; always lurking threat to Mag 7; profitability not yet proven.
Export controls lifted June 12; IPO timing uncertain as company navigates government interference; could still IPO by EOY if regulatory climate stabilizes and numbers hold.
Gigantic private fundraising adds to capital drain; Cramer says it had better come public soon given endless cash needs.
Cited as the AI monetization benchmark Amazon's data center spend is measured against.
Frontier AI model targeted by China's Kimmy K3; IPO timing in question amid cost pressures and geopolitical headwinds.
